title: Crispy Polish Salmon Salad
description: A light, comforting salad featuring air‑fried salmon, crisp greens, and a touch of honey‑mustard dressing—perfect for a sick day or a quick lunch.
instructions: Pat the salmon fillet dry with paper towels.
Brush the salmon with olive oil and season with smoked paprika, salt, and pepper.
Place the salmon in the air fryer basket and cook for 10–12 minutes, or until the skin is crisp and the flesh flakes easily.
While the salmon cooks, toss the arugula, radishes, cucumber, and red onion in a large bowl.
Whisk together the apple cider vinegar, Dijon mustard, honey, and lemon juice to make the dressing.
Drizzle the dressing over the salad and toss to coat.
Once the salmon is done, let it rest for 2 minutes, then flake it into large chunks.
Gently fold the salmon into the salad, taking care not to break the flakes.
Sprinkle chopped dill over the top for a fresh herb finish.
Serve the salad immediately, optionally with a side of crusty rye or a light bread roll if desired.
